raw_response: arm bent, chest height, strong, forward, upward
thinking:
Okay, let's break this down. The user wants me to imagine being a right-handed person in an empty room, with a palm-sized object on a table. The action is to "hurl" the object. I need to figure out the most natural way a native English speaker would perform this.

First, "hurl" implies a forceful throw. So the force level is probably strong. The arm's initial state: when throwing, you usually have your arm bent to build up momentum. If the arm were extended, you might be in the middle of the motion already. So arm bent makes sense.

Hand position before the action: Hurling would require the hand to be at a higher position to generate power. Chest height seems right because you'd hold the object there and then swing forward. Shoulder height might be too high, and head height even more so. Near ground is too low.

Vertical direction: Throwing usually involves an upward motion to get the object airborne. So upward movement. Horizontal direction is forward, as you're tossing the object in that direction.

Putting it all together: Strong force, arm bent initially, hand at chest height, moving upward and forward. The sentence needs to include all these elements concisely using the given options.
